objective.subject: English
objective.statement: Use fronted adverbials to add time, place or manner detail at the start of a sentence, followed by a comma.
misconceptions: Adds a comma after any first word rather than after the whole fronted adverbial.
Confuses a fronted adverbial with the subject of the sentence.
Uses a sentence opener that does not say when, where or how.
objective.parent_explanation: Can start sentences with fronted adverbials and place the comma correctly.
objective.teacher_evidence: Identifies fronted adverbials, uses commas after full opener phrases and builds grammatically complete sentences.

activities.title: I can start a sentence with an adverbial and use a comma.
activities.prompt: Move the detail phrase to the front of the sentence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Which phrase tells us when the action happened?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Check adverbial meaning before moving phrases to the front.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Some phrases tell us when, where or how something happens.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three phrase cards glow as time, place or manner details.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Move the detail phrase to the front of the sentence.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Introduce fronting as a sentence design choice.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: We can move the detail to the front to guide the reader.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A sentence train swaps the adverbial carriage to the front.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: After sunset, the explorers returned to camp.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model comma placement after the complete fronted adverbial.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: After sunset is the fronted adverbial. The comma comes after the whole phrase.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Comma gate drops after the phrase 'After sunset'.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Build a sentence that starts with 'Behind the waterfall'.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise constructing a complete sentence from opener and main clause.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Put the opener first, add a comma, then finish the main clause.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Phrase tiles snap into opener, comma and main-clause slots.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Where should the comma go in 'With careful steps the team crossed the bridge'?
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Find the whole fronted adverbial before placing the comma.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A draggable comma moves across the sentence.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Teach Rex why the comma goes after the whole opener.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Use explanation to secure comma reasoning.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: I put the comma after the first word. Can you show me why that is not enough?
teaching_sequence.visual_model: The companion compares two comma placements.

manipulative.purpose: Make sentence structure movable and visible before independent writing.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Learners sort adverbials, drag openers to the front, place commas and compare sentence effects.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Keyboard tile movement, audio sentence reading and low-sensory static sentence tracks.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use quiet sentence-track colours and no fast tile spinning.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Use instant tile movement and static comma markers.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Read sentences aloud with a pause after the fronted adverbial.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Chunk sentences into opener, comma and main-clause panels.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Show only one edit decision at a time.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow sentence replay and tile undo before submitting.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Use editing-studio language: the sentence is being tuned.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Preteach when, where, how, phrase, comma and clause with examples.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Sort time, place and manner adverbials before sentence construction.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: phrase card + time/place/manner target + distractor phrases
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Use age-appropriate vocabulary and avoid ambiguous openers early.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Move adverbial phrases to the front and preserve sentence meaning.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: main clause + adverbial phrase + tile order task

variant_blueprints.purpose: Place commas after the full fronted adverbial, not after one word.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: sentence without comma + draggable comma positions
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Include multi-word openers and single-word adverb openers separately.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Choose an opener that improves flow, setting or pace.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short paragraph + candidate openers + effect prompt
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Teacher review needed for style judgements.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Maintain comma and opener recognition through spaced review.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: mixed short sentences from review queue
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Keep retrieval items short for warm-up use.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Adds a comma after any first word rather than after the whole fronted adverbial.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner places comma after the first word of a multi-word opener.
repair_sequence: Read the opener aloud as one chunk.
Move the comma after the bracketed phrase.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner places the comma after a new multi-word opener.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Confuses a fronted adverbial with the subject of the sentence.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: Learner identifies the noun doing the action as the opener.
repair_sequence: Ask when, where or how the action happens.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: Learner distinguishes opener detail from the subject in a new sentence.

feedback.companion_prompt: Teach Rex why the comma goes after the whole opener.
feedback.parent_summary: Can start sentences with fronted adverbials and place the comma correctly.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Identifies fronted adverbials, uses commas after full opener phrases and builds grammatically complete sentences.
next_actions: Interleave fronted adverbials with expanded noun phrases.
Use paragraph-level writing once sentence editing is secure.
Add retrieval edits to warm-up practice.

body.prompt: Place the comma: After sunset the explorers returned to camp.
body.text: After sunset the explorers returned to camp.
choices: After, sunset the explorers returned to camp.
After sunset, the explorers returned to camp.
After sunset the, explorers returned to camp.

expected_answer.value: After sunset, the explorers returned to camp.

questions.explanation: The fronted adverbial is 'After sunset', so the comma goes after the whole phrase.

body.prompt: What type of adverbial is 'Behind the waterfall'?

questions.explanation: 'Behind the waterfall' tells where the action happens, so it is a place adverbial.

body.prompt: Build the sentence with the opener at the front.

hints: Add a comma before the main clause.
questions.explanation: The sentence is 'With careful steps, the team crossed the bridge.'

reward_payload.explanation: Can start sentences with fronted adverbials and place the comma correctly.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Teach Rex why the comma goes after the whole opener.
